nodes.text: Good evening. Let's take a few minutes to reflect on your day — not to judge, just to see clearly.
If you had to describe today in one word, what would it be?
options: Productive
Frustrating
Mixed
Tough

nodes.text: When something went well today, what do you think most contributed?
options: I prepared or anticipated the situation
The team or others stepped up
A bit of luck or timing
I adjusted quickly when things changed

nodes.text: And when you faced a small setback or delay, how did you respond?
options: I looked for what I could still control
I felt frustrated but kept going
I relied on someone else to fix it
I let it pass and focused on the next thing

nodes.text: When things felt difficult today, what was your first instinct?
options: Figure out what was within my control
Wait for someone to step in or clarify
Push through on my own
Feel stuck or overwhelmed

nodes.text: Looking back, was there a moment where you did make a choice — even a small one — that shaped how the rest of the day went?
options: Yes, I can think of one
Maybe, but it didn't change much
Not really, things just happened
I'm not sure

nodes.text: You see your own hand in how today unfolded. That's agency — not everything went your way, but you stayed in the driver's seat. Even noticing that is a form of power.

A tough day pulls our attention outward — to what others did or didn't do. Fair enough. But somewhere in there, you made a call. Maybe it was how you responded to an email, or whether you took a breath before reacting. Those small choices are real. And they're yours.
Let's shift from *how you handled things* to *what you gave and received* today.
Think about an interaction today. Were you mostly giving or mostly expecting?
options: I helped someone without being asked
I taught or explained something to a colleague
I found myself waiting for recognition or support
I felt others weren't pulling their weight

nodes.text: What motivated you to step up like that?
options: It just felt like the right thing to do
I wanted to make the team's work easier
I expected it would be noticed or appreciated
I was filling a gap no one else saw

nodes.text: When you think about what you received today — recognition, thanks, support — what stands out?
options: I got less than I deserved
I got about what I expected
I didn't need much today
I received more than I gave

nodes.text: You gave something today that wasn't strictly required. That's the quiet fuel of good teams — small acts of citizenship that keep the whole thing moving. It's not about being a hero; it's about being a contributor.

It's easy to feel shortchanged at work — we all do sometimes. But notice: that feeling is about what you *deserved*. What if we flip the lens? Tomorrow, what's one thing you could give that would make someone else's day a little easier?
Now let's widen the view. From *what you gave* to *who else was in the picture*.
When you think about today's biggest challenge, who comes to mind?
options: Just me — it was my problem to solve
My immediate team — we were all affected
A specific colleague who had it harder than me
The customer or end user we're serving

nodes.text: Did you feel that carrying the load alone made things better or heavier?
options: Better — I like being responsible for my own work
Heavier — I wished I had more support
It was necessary — no one else could do it
I'm not sure

nodes.text: In what way did considering others change how you approached the day?
options: I adjusted my priorities to help the team
I felt more connected to our shared purpose
I was more patient with someone who was struggling
It didn't change much — I still focused on my own tasks

nodes.text: You carried a lot today. That's strength, but it can also be lonely. The next step isn't to stop being capable — it's to notice that your work sits inside a bigger story. Who else might have a stake in what you do?
You saw beyond your own desk today. That's the shift from *my work* to *our work*. It doesn't erase stress, but it puts it in a larger context — and that often makes the load feel lighter.

Today you leaned {axis1.dominant} in terms of agency, and {axis2.dominant} in terms of contribution. Your lens was mostly {axis3.dominant}.

{summary_reflection}
That's it for tonight. Tomorrow is a fresh day. Sleep well.
